The 2011 race took place at [Tokyo Racecourse](/wiki/Tokyo_Racecourse "Tokyo Racecourse") due to the [2011 Tohoku earthquake and tsunami](/wiki/2011_Tohoku_earthquake_and_tsunami "2011 Tohoku earthquake and tsunami")

The **Satsuki Shō** (皐月賞) is a [Grade 1](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") for three-year-old [thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") [colts](/wiki/Colt_(horse) "Colt (horse)") and [fillies](/wiki/Filly "Filly") run over a distance of 2,000 metres (approximately 1+1⁄4 mile) at the [Nakayama Racecourse](/wiki/Nakayama_Racecourse "Nakayama Racecourse"), [Funabashi, Chiba](/wiki/Funabashi,_Chiba "Funabashi, Chiba"), in April.

It was first run in 1939 and is the Japanese equivalent of the English [2,000 Guineas](/wiki/2,000_Guineas_Stakes "2,000 Guineas Stakes"). (Note that the original 2,000 Guineas is run at 1,609 metres, or one mile, about two furlongs shorter than the Satsuki Shō.)
